web_research_sources.title: Accident Reconstruction Visualization & Animation
web_research_sources.key_findings: Multiple camera angles (overhead, driver POV, witness view, slow-motion impact) essential for forensic animation

web_research_sources.title: Creating A Bird's-Eye Camera View In Blender
web_research_sources.key_findings: Position camera above scene, use orthographic for technical views or perspective for cinematic

web_research_sources.title: Blender Camera Tutorial
web_research_sources.key_findings: Adjust clip start/end to prevent clipping artifacts, wider FOV for context shots

web_research_sources.title: Fix CAMERA CLIPPING Issues in Blender
web_research_sources.key_findings: Dark renders inside vehicles caused by clip start too low; increase to 0.1-1m, regulate clip end to prevent geometry inversion

web_research_sources.title: Blender Camera Settings - Field of View
web_research_sources.key_findings: Lower focal length = wider FOV; security cameras use wide angle (4-8mm equivalent); use fisheye for extreme wide angle

scene1.name: Scene 1 - Basic Intersection

cameras.name: BirdEye
issues: Height 200.5m excessive for 100-200m scene; should be 30-50m max
Focal length 50mm good but could be 35-50mm range

cameras.name: DriverPOV
issues: Location is too far away (160m X, -80m Y); should be ~1-3m from vehicle center
Height 70.5m is unrealistic for inside-vehicle; should be ~1.2-1.5m above road

cameras.name: WideAngle
issues: Position too far and high; should be 20-30m away, 5m height, 30-45° angle to intersection

scene1.recommendations: All cameras are scaled too large/high. Scene likely uses scaled coordinates. Need to audit actual vehicle positions and adjust accordingly.

scene2.name: Scene 2 - Pedestrian Crossing

cameras.name: BirdEye
issues: Height 200m excessive; should be 25-35m for 100m² scene
Focal length 50mm adequate but 35mm might better frame scene

cameras.name: SightLine
issues: Position far outside scene bounds (-120, 140 vs scene -50 to 50); needs repositioning
Could use 28mm for wider field of view

cameras.name: WideAngle
issues: Position also outside bounds; needs repositioning within 70-unit perimeter

scene2.recommendations: Cameras positioned outside scene bounds. Need to reposition within ~70 units of scene center. Consider witness POV camera instead of SightLine.

scene3.name: Scene 3 - Highway Collision

cameras.name: BirdEye
issues: Height 480.5m excessive for 240m×120m scene; should be 60-80m
50mm focal length acceptable but 35-40mm better for wide highway view

cameras.name: DriverPOV
issues: Position way outside scene (384, -192 vs bounds ±120/60); position should be within vehicle on road
Height 168.5m unrealistic; should be ~1.2-1.5m for truck/car cab

cameras.name: WideAngle
issues: Position outside bounds (288, 192); reposition to 30-40m away at 5-8m height

scene3.recommendations: Severe scaling issue. Cameras scaled 4x larger than scene. Clip end may need increase to 5000+ given distances. All positions need complete recalculation.

scene4.name: Scene 4 - Parking Lot

cameras.name: BirdEye
issues: Height 10m appropriate for parking lot (150m² scene)
Focal length 50mm good; could use 35-40mm for slightly wider view of entire lot

cameras.name: SecurityCam
issues: Focal length 50mm too narrow for security camera (should be 4-8mm equiv. 100-170° FOV)
Rotation -30° tilt good but needs wider lens
Position (-10, -10, 5) good, but needs wide-angle focal length

cameras.name: WideAngle
issues: Position good, angle good, but focal length could be 28-35mm for wider establishing shot

scene4.recommendations: Scene 4 is well-scaled. SecurityCam needs wide-angle lens (5-8mm focal length = 170°+ FOV). BirdEye and WideAngle focal lengths could be reduced to 35mm and 28mm respectively.

problems_found: Severe camera scaling issues in scenes 1, 2, 3 - cameras positioned 4-200x too far from subjects
DriverPOV cameras in unrealistic positions (70m+ height) instead of inside vehicle cab (~1.2m)
BirdEye cameras at extreme heights (200-480m) instead of practical heights (30-80m)
SecurityCam in scene 4 uses 50mm focal length instead of security camera wide-angle (5-8mm)
All cameras have clip_start=0.1m which is fine but clip_end=1000m may be insufficient for scaled scenes
No camera auto-framing to scene geometry - manual positioning leads to inconsistent framing
Camera naming inconsistent - some are generic names, need standardized naming per scene

BirdEye.description: Overhead view showing spatial relationships and evidence markers
BirdEye.notes: Position directly above scene center, looking straight down. Height depends on scene size. Use 35mm for ~80-120° horizontal FOV.

DriverPOV.description: Dashboard view - shows what driver could see, sight line analysis
DriverPOV.notes: Position inside vehicle cab at driver's eye height. Slight downward tilt (10° pitch). CRITICAL: Ensure cabin has lighting or add interior lights. Use 35mm for human-realistic ~50° FOV.

WideEstablishing.description: Overview context shot - professional presentation of scene
WideEstablishing.notes: Position 20-30m away from scene at ~5m height, angled 30-45° down toward impact point. Use 28mm for ~75° horizontal FOV.

SecurityCam.description: Fixed angle security camera - simulates actual security footage
SecurityCam.notes: Fixed position at 3-5m height on nearby structure. Use 6mm focal length for ~170° FOV (realistic security dome). Tilt down 35-45°.

implementation_status: Script created and ready for testing
next_steps: Apply v12_cameras.py to scene 1 and render test images
Verify camera framing captures all mesh objects
Test render quality for each camera angle
Iterate focal lengths based on render tests
Apply to scenes 2, 3, 4 and render complete test suite
